task_completion.description: Sketch the left and right outer arcs with correct radii and center distance.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a 2D sketch in SolveSpace showing two outer circles/arcs. There should be a dimension of 20 (radius) for the left arc, 15 (radius) for the right arc, and a horizontal distance constraint of 100 between their centers.

task_completion.description: Connect the outer arcs with tangent lines.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for two lines connecting the top and bottom of the outer arcs. Tangent constraint icons (a line touching a circle) should be visible where the lines meet the arcs, forming the smooth outer profile of the connecting rod.

task_completion.description: Sketch the concentric inner holes with correct diameters.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for two inner circles sharing the same centers as the outer arcs. The left inner circle should have a dimension of 20 (diameter) or 10 (radius). The right inner circle should have a dimension of 15 (diameter) or 7.5 (radius).

task_completion.description: Extrude the 2D profile to a thickness of 10mm.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look at the property browser or 3D view. The sketch should be extruded into a 3D solid, and the extrusion depth/thickness should be explicitly set to 10mm.

task_completion.description: Save the SolveSpace model to the specified path.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a 'Save As' dialog or a file manager window showing the file 'connecting_rod.slvs' being saved in the '/home/ga/Documents/SolveSpace/' directory.

task_completion.description: Export the model as an STL file to the specified path.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for an export dialog or terminal/file manager confirming 'connecting_rod.stl' has been created in the '/home/ga/Documents/SolveSpace/' directory.

integrity.description: The agent must use the SolveSpace GUI to create the model.
integrity.visual_evidence: Check that screenshots show the SolveSpace application interface being actively used (drawing tools, constraint panel, property browser) rather than just running a script in the terminal.

integrity.description: The 3D model must be genuinely constructed from the sketch.
integrity.visual_evidence: The 3D view should display the connecting rod with the exact sketched constraints visible in the history/property panel, not an imported pre-made STL or a generic cylinder.

integrity.description: The agent must not create dummy files to bypass the task.
integrity.visual_evidence: Check that the agent did not simply use 'touch connecting_rod.slvs' or 'echo > connecting_rod.stl' in the terminal without actually exporting them from SolveSpace.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The final 3D model should have exact bounding box extents of [135.0, 40.0, 10.0] mm. The volume of the correct solid is approximately 39777.42 mm^3 (acceptable range: 36000.0 to 42000.0 mm^3). The topology must consist of a single solid body with exactly two through-holes, resulting in an Euler characteristic of -2. Both the .slvs and .stl files must be located in /home/ga/Documents/SolveSpace/.
